---
title: "Zero-Shot Country Matching Explorer"
description: "Type a country name with typos, aliases, or alternate languages and see how the Matcher resolves it to a canonical entity — no training required."
date: today
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    embed-resources: true
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
---

This notebook demonstrates zero-shot entity matching using the Novel Entity Matcher.
Given a set of country entities with common aliases, it resolves free-text queries
(including typos and foreign names) to the correct canonical entity without any training.

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| cache: true
import pandas as pd
from novelentitymatcher import Matcher

entities = [
    {"id": "DE", "name": "Germany", "aliases": ["Deutschland", "Bundesrepublik"]},
    {"id": "FR", "name": "France", "aliases": ["Frankreich", "La France"]},
    {"id": "US", "name": "United States", "aliases": ["USA", "America"]},
    {"id": "JP", "name": "Japan", "aliases": ["Nihon", "Nippon"]},
    {"id": "CN", "name": "China", "aliases": ["Zhongguo"]},
    {"id": "BR", "name": "Brazil", "aliases": ["Brasil"]},
    {"id": "IN", "name": "India", "aliases": ["Bharat"]},
    {"id": "GB", "name": "United Kingdom", "aliases": ["UK", "Britain", "England"]},
    {"id": "AU", "name": "Australia", "aliases": ["Oz"]},
    {"id": "CA", "name": "Canada", "aliases": ["Canuck Land"]},
]
```

```{python}
#| label: fit
#| cache: true
matcher = Matcher(entities=entities, mode="zero-shot")
matcher.fit()
```

```{python}
#| label: match-examples
#| cache: true
queries = [
    "Deutchland",
    "America",
    "Frankreich",
    "Nihon",
    "UK",
    "Brasil",
    "Bharat",
    "Canuck Land",
    "Zhongguo",
    "Oz",
]

results = []
for q in queries:
    r = matcher.match(q, top_k=3)
    batch = r if isinstance(r, list) else [r]
    for entry in batch:
        entry["query"] = q
    results.extend(batch)

df = pd.DataFrame(results)
df
```

```{python}
#| label: summary
correct = 0
total = len(queries)
for q in queries:
    r = matcher.match(q)
    entry = r if isinstance(r, dict) else r
    is_correct = isinstance(entry, dict) and entry.get("id") in {
        e["id"] for e in entities if q.lower() in [e["name"].lower()] + [a.lower() for a in e["aliases"]] + [e["id"].lower()]
    }
    if is_correct:
        correct += 1

print(f"Accuracy: {correct}/{total} ({correct/total:.0%})")
```
